Light Refresh achieves approximately 78% of the requested program while minimizing demolition, new partitions, and schedule impact. Remaining differences are fit variance, a field-verification item.

AI Analysis

This 'Light Refresh' scenario is ideal for the tenant seeking a quick, budget-friendly solution with immediate occupancy. It minimizes disruption and capital expenditure by reusing most existing infrastructure, primarily focusing on aesthetic upgrades like new finishes and furniture. While it may not perfectly optimize the layout for modern professional services workflows, it provides a functional and refreshed environment. Brokers can highlight the significant cost savings, rapid deployment, and reduced risk associated with this approach, making it attractive for tenants prioritizing speed and financial prudence. Light Refresh achieves approximately 78% of the requested program while minimizing demolition, new partitions, and schedule impact. Remaining differences are fit variance, a field-verification item.

Program Breakdown
  • 12x Private Office (1,440 sq ft)
  • 80x Workstation (8,000 sq ft)
  • 2x Conference Room (6-8 person) (400 sq ft)
  • 3x Huddle Room (2-4 person) (240 sq ft)
  • 1x Reception/Waiting (250 sq ft)
  • 1x Breakroom/Kitchenette (350 sq ft)
  • 1x Open Collaboration Zone (600 sq ft)
  • 2x Storage/IT (300 sq ft)
  • 0x Circulation/Support (6,220 sq ft)
Budget Breakdown
Category Low Mid High
Construction $421,800 $632,700 $888,000
FF&E (Furniture, Fixtures & Equipment) $126,540 $189,810 $266,400
IT / AV (Technology & Audio-Visual) $70,300 $105,450 $148,000
Soft Costs (Design, Permits, etc.) $56,240 $84,360 $118,400
TI Allowance Credit $28,120 $42,180 $59,200
Project Schedule
Phase Duration Description
1. Design & Space Planning 2–3 wks Updated furniture layout, finish selections, and minor partition modifications. Minimal permit requirements.
2. Cosmetic Demolition 1–2 wks Remove existing finishes, flooring, and fixtures only. No structural or MEP demolition required.
3. Finishes & Refresh 4–5 wks New flooring, paint, lighting upgrades, and minor millwork. Reuse all existing walls and infrastructure.
4. FF&E & Technology 2–3 wks Furniture installation, minor IT/AV updates, and signage.
5. Punch List & Move-In 1 wks Final walk-through, punch list items, and occupancy.
Total estimated timeline: 8–16 weeks (2–4 months)

Moderate Build-Out achieves approximately 92% of the requested program through targeted reconfiguration and selective reuse of existing conditions.

AI Analysis

The 'Moderate Build-Out' scenario offers a strong balance between cost, time, and functional optimization for the professional services tenant. It allows for significant improvements to the space's efficiency and alignment with modern work styles by reconfiguring the core layout and introducing more varied work settings. While involving a higher budget and longer timeline than a light refresh, it delivers a more tailored and productive environment. Brokers can present this as the 'sweet spot' – achieving most of the tenant's programmatic needs and enhancing employee experience without the full expense and duration of a complete demolition. Moderate Build-Out achieves approximately 92% of the requested program through targeted reconfiguration and selective reuse of existing conditions.

Program Breakdown
  • 8x Private Office (960 sq ft)
  • 84x Workstation (8,400 sq ft)
  • 1x Conference Room (10-12 person) (300 sq ft)
  • 2x Conference Room (6-8 person) (400 sq ft)
  • 4x Huddle Room (2-4 person) (320 sq ft)
  • 4x Phone Booth (160 sq ft)
  • 1x Reception/Waiting (200 sq ft)
  • 1x Large Breakroom/Cafe (600 sq ft)
  • 2x Open Collaboration Zone (900 sq ft)
  • 1x Storage/IT (200 sq ft)
  • 0x Circulation/Support (6,060 sq ft)
Budget Breakdown
Category Low Mid High
Construction $843,600 $1,221,000 $1,642,800
FF&E (Furniture, Fixtures & Equipment) $253,080 $366,300 $492,840
IT / AV (Technology & Audio-Visual) $140,600 $203,500 $273,800
Soft Costs (Design, Permits, etc.) $112,480 $162,800 $219,040
TI Allowance Credit $56,240 $81,400 $109,520
Project Schedule
Phase Duration Description
1. Design & Permitting 3–5 wks Full space planning, construction documents, and permit submission. Some structural review may be required.
2. Selective Demolition 2–3 wks Demolish targeted walls and reconfigure layout. Reuse existing MEP infrastructure where possible to reduce cost and time.
3. Construction 6–9 wks New framing, drywall, MEP modifications, flooring, ceilings, and finishes throughout.
4. FF&E & IT/AV 2–3 wks Full furniture package, technology infrastructure, and audio-visual systems.
5. Punch List & Move-In 1–2 wks Final inspections, certificate of occupancy, and move-in coordination.
Total estimated timeline: 14–26 weeks (3–6 months)

Full Transformation achieves approximately 100% of the requested program by preserving shell/core and redesigning flexible interior zones for best long-term fit.

AI Analysis

The 'Full Transformation' scenario provides the ultimate long-term solution, delivering a fully customized and optimized space that perfectly aligns with the tenant's brand and operational needs. By starting from a base building condition, it eliminates all constraints of existing layouts, allowing for maximum efficiency and a superior employee experience. While this option entails the highest cost and longest timeline, it offers the best return on investment for long-term tenancy, fostering productivity and talent retention. Brokers can emphasize the strategic value of a purpose-built environment, showcasing it as an investment in the company's future, culture, and operational excellence. Full Transformation achieves approximately 100% of the requested program by preserving shell/core and redesigning flexible interior zones for best long-term fit.

Program Breakdown
  • 6x Private Office (720 sq ft)
  • 86x Workstation (8,600 sq ft)
  • 1x Boardroom (14+ person) (400 sq ft)
  • 2x Conference Room (8-10 person) (500 sq ft)
  • 6x Huddle Room (2-4 person) (480 sq ft)
  • 6x Phone Booth (240 sq ft)
  • 1x Reception/Waiting (250 sq ft)
  • 1x Large Cafe/Lounge (800 sq ft)
  • 3x Open Collaboration Zone (1,200 sq ft)
  • 1x Wellness Room (150 sq ft)
  • 1x IT/Server Room (150 sq ft)
  • 1x Storage (100 sq ft)
  • 0x Circulation/Support (4,910 sq ft)
Budget Breakdown
Category Low Mid High
Construction $1,332,000 $1,798,200 $2,386,500
FF&E (Furniture, Fixtures & Equipment) $399,600 $539,460 $715,950
IT / AV (Technology & Audio-Visual) $222,000 $299,700 $397,750
Soft Costs (Design, Permits, etc.) $177,600 $239,760 $318,200
TI Allowance Credit $88,800 $119,880 $159,100
Project Schedule
Phase Duration Description
1. Design & Permitting 4–7 wks Complete architectural and engineering drawings, MEP design, structural review, and full permit package. Landlord approval required.
2. Full Demolition 3–5 wks Complete gut demolition — all walls, ceilings, flooring, and MEP systems removed to base building condition.
3. MEP Rough-In 4–6 wks New mechanical, electrical, and plumbing systems installed from scratch. Includes HVAC, sprinkler, and data infrastructure.
4. Construction & Finishes 8–11 wks Framing, drywall, specialty finishes, custom millwork, and all architectural elements.
5. FF&E & IT/AV 3–4 wks Full furniture package, comprehensive technology infrastructure, audio-visual systems, and signage program.
6. Punch List & Move-In 2–3 wks Final inspections, commissioning, certificate of occupancy, and phased move-in.
Total estimated timeline: 22–40 weeks (5–9 months)
